claim: Curated map of Syn3A SBML species IDs (BiGG-style M_<id>_<c|e>) to canonical SMILES for the small molecules that appear in the iMB155 metabolic network. Hand-built because the Syn3A SBML carries no bqbiol:is cross-references (verified: zero rdf:resource tags) and the MetaNetX / BiGG / UniProt APIs are blocked by the sandbox allowlist. Covers 48 unique BiGG IDs x 88 species_id rows (c + e compartments), focusing on the inorganics, 20 proteogenic amino acids + homocysteine, nucleobases, simple sugars, glycerol, and core central-carbon + fermentation end products. Enables MACEOffExtractor to populate real BDE-derived kcat aggregates for the reactions whose substrates are fully in this map.

schema.bigg_id: BiGG canonical identifier without compartment suffix (e.g. atp)

coverage.estimated_reaction_coverage: partial: roughly 15-25 percent of iMB155 reactions have ALL substrates mapped (amino-acid metabolism, glycolysis entry, fermentation, nucleobase salvage). Central-carbon mid-stream (e.g. f6p, fdp, g3p, dhap, 13dpg, 3pg, 2pg, pep), nucleotide-phosphate species (atp, adp, amp, gtp, gdp, gmp, ...), cofactors (nad / nadp / fad / coa), and complex lipids remain unmapped.

categories_covered: inorganics (h, h2o, pi, ppi, co2, o2, nh3, na1, k, cl, ca2, mg2)
simple sugars (glc__D, gam, man) + glyc + glyc3p + gam6p
C2/C3 organic acids (ac, acald, pyr, lac__L, pep, for)
categories_excluded: tRNA species (alatrna, argtrna, ... and the trnaXxx stems): macromolecules — MACE-OFF is a small-molecule backend and cannot compute BDE for them
Protein / DNA / RNA / biomass / ACP / PdhC / dUTPase: macromolecular placeholders in the SBML
Complex lipids (pc, pg, pa, clpn, sm, 12dgr, 1ag3p, galfur12dgr, cdpdag, tag): multi-variant acyl-chain species; need chain-specific SMILES to be meaningful

Central-carbon phosphorylated intermediates (f6p, fdp, 13dpg, 3pg, 2pg, g3p, dhap, g6p, g1p): deferred pending confirmed source of canonical SMILES (each has a well-known SMILES; deferred to avoid propagating a typo through the MACE BDE pipeline)
Nucleotide mono/di/triphosphates (atp, adp, amp, gtp, ...): deferred for the same reason
Cofactors (coa, nad/nadh, nadp/nadph, fad, fmn, amet, ahcys, thmpp, pydx5p, ribflv): deferred for the same reason

source_detail: Hand-curated from standard BiGG identifiers. SMILES for amino acids follow IUPAC (L-stereochemistry specified as @ / @@ at the C-alpha). Inorganics use standard charged/uncharged forms. Cross-referenced against the SBML species list via cell_sim/layer3_reactions/sbml_parser.py::parse_sbml. No network fetch at build time.
caveats: Partial coverage: 48/226 unique metabolites mapped. The deferred categories (phosphorylated central-carbon intermediates, nucleotide phosphates, cofactors) cover many more reactions — adding them in a follow-up session would raise reaction coverage past 50 percent.

SMILES are canonical but not necessarily the EXACT tautomer or protonation state iMB155 assumes. MACE-OFF's BDE calculation is relatively insensitive to protonation (bond dissociation is a homolytic-cleavage energy, computed on the neutral bonded form), so minor protonation disagreements should not materially affect aggregated kcat estimates.
Stereochemistry is encoded where applicable (L-amino acids, alpha-D-glucose, D-mannose, L-lactate, glycerol-3-P). Uncharged forms used throughout; cofactor metal coordination not represented.
Extending this map is a straightforward next-session task. Every added BiGG id increases MACE-OFF reaction coverage roughly linearly until lipid / cofactor bottlenecks dominate.
